items.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slump as Wall Street looks to big bank earnings
items.description: US stock futures were mostly flat as investors took stock after a strong rebound session from Friday's tariff-fueled free fall.

items.title: Banks kick off earnings, Fed Chair Powell speaks: What to Watch
items.description: Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton takes a look at the top stories for investors to watch on Tuesday, Oct. 14. Earnings season kicks off, with JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), Goldman Sachs (GS), Citi (C), BlackRock (BLK), and Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) all reporting results. Federal Reserve commentary is expected from Fed Chair Jerome Powell, Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman, Fed Governor Christopher Waller, and Boston Fed President Susan Collins. The Small Business Association's (SBA's) National Federation of Independent Business (NFIB) September Small Business Optimism Index will be published in the morning. Economists are expecting a slight decrease from the prior month. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

items.title: Review & Preview: A Quick Recovery
items.description: Stocks sank on Friday on an apparent escalation of the trade war between Washington and Beijing.  On Monday, they bounced much of the way back after President Donald Trump said over the weekend, “Don’t worry about China.”  The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 588 points, or 1.3%, while the S&P 500 rose 1.6% and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 2.2%.

items.title: Markets Rebound Nicely Ahead of Q3 Earnings Season
items.description: Even though we've seen more than a dozen companies already officially report Q3 earnings results this period, Q3 earnings season really kicks off with the release of earnings from some of the biggest banks on Wall Street.

items.title: JPMorgan Outlines $10 Billion Plan in Private Sector’s Latest U.S. Investment Under Trump
items.description: The bank has a 10-year plan to facilitate and finance roughly $1.5 trillion in support of critical industries.
